Worship is the purpose of the act—that is, a perfection returning to it, not to the Agent, as previously mentioned.

It appears from the restriction (القصر) in the verse through negation and exception that Allah has no concern for those who do not worship Him, as is also indicated by His saying: "Say, ‘My Lord would not care for you were it not for your prayer’."

His saying, the Exalted: ما أُرِيدُ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ رِزْقٍ وَما أُرِيدُ أَنْ يُطْعِمُونِ "I do not desire any provision from them, nor do I desire that they should feed Me." (51:57). Feeding (الإطعام) — the act of giving food so that it may be tasted and eaten. He, the Exalted, said: وَالَّذِي هُوَ يُطْعِمُنِي وَيَسْقِينِ "...and who feeds me and waters me" (26:79), and said: الَّذِي أَطْعَمَهُمْ مِنْ جُوعٍ "...who has fed them [and saved them] from hunger" (106:4). Thus, the mention of feeding after provision (الرزق) is of the nature of mentioning the specific after the general, because a special concern relates to it—namely, that nourishment is the most extensive of the needs of man and others, and the lowliest of them, being preceded by hunger and followed by excretion.

It is said: What is meant by provision (الرزق) is the provision of the servants. The meaning would be: I do not desire from them that they provide for My servants whom I provide for, nor do I desire that they feed Me Myself.

It is also said: What is meant by feeding (الإطعام) is presenting food to Him, just as a servant presents food to his master or a helper to the one he serves. Thus, what is meant by provision would be the acquisition of the source of provision, and by feeding, the presentation of what they have acquired. The meaning would be: I do not desire from them a provision that they acquire for Me so that I may be provided by it, nor do I desire from them that they present to Me that which I would be provided by and eat.

His saying, the Exalted: إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الرَّزَّاقُ ذُو الْقُوَّةِ الْمَتِينُ "Indeed it is Allah who is the All-provider, the Possessor of all power, the Authoritative." (51:58). This is a justification for His saying: "I do not desire any provision from them..." etc. The transition (الالتفات) in the verse from speaking in the singular to the third person (الغيبة) is to terminate the justification at the Majestic Name (اسم الجلالة) from which everything begins and to which everything returns. It is as if He said: I do not desire provision from them because I am the Provider, because I am Allah, blessed be His Name.

The expression "the All-provider (الرزاق)"—an intensive form (اسم مبالغة)—where the apparent phrasing might have been "Indeed Allah is the Provider," is to indicate that since He, the Exalted, is the sole Provider, He is the All-provider due to the multitude of those He provides for. Thus, the verse is similar to His saying: وَما أَنَا بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ "...and I am not a tyrant to My servants" (50:29).

"Possessor of Power (ذو القوة)" is among His names, the Exalted, meaning the Powerful, but it is more emphatic than "the Powerful (القوي)." "The Authoritative (المتين)" is also among His names, the Exalted, meaning the Strong.

The use of these three names is to indicate the restriction of provision to Him, the Exalted, and that no weakness overcomes Him in delivering provision to those who receive it, despite their great number.

His saying, the Exalted: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذَنُوباً مِثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحابِهِمْ فَلا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ "Indeed the lot of those who do wrong shall be like the lot of their companions [in the past]; so let them not ask Me to hasten [it]." (51:59). The lot (الذنوب) — the portion or share. Hastening (الاستعجال) — the act of seeking speed and urging it. The verse is a derivation from His saying: "I did not create the jinn and the humans except that they may worship Me" by way of its necessary implication.

The meaning is: Since these wrongdoers do not worship Allah, and He has no concern for them, and no felicity from Him encompasses them, then they have a portion (نصيب) of punishment like the portion of their companions from the past perished nations. So let them not ask Me to hasten the punishment for them, nor let them say: "When is this promise, if you are truthful?" and "When is the Day of Retribution?"

In the verse, there is a transition (التفات) from the third person to speaking in the singular. This is, in reality, a return from the context of the third person in His saying: "Indeed it is Allah who is the All-provider..." etc., to speaking in the singular which was in His saying: "I did not create..." etc., because the discourse is derived from it.

His saying, the Exalted: فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ يَوْمِهِمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ "So woe to the faithless on account of their day which they are promised!" (51:60). This is a derivation from His saying: "Indeed the lot of those who do wrong..." etc., and an alert that this lot is certain for them on the Day of Resurrection, even if it is possible that some of it be assigned to them [in this world]. It is a day in which they have nothing but woe and destruction, and it is their promised day.

In the substitution of His saying in the previous verse "those who do wrong (لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا)" with His saying in this verse "the faithless (لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)," there is an alert that what is meant by wrongdoing (الظلم) is the wrongdoing of disbelief (كفر).

(Narrative Discussion)

In al-Majma': It is narrated with a chain of transmission from Mujahid who said: Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) came out wearing a turban and wrapped in his shirt, and said: "When فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَما أَنْتَ بِمَلُومٍ (So disregard them, for you will not be blamed) was revealed, there was not one of us but was certain of destruction—when it was said to the Prophet: 'So disregard them.' But when وَذَكِّرْ فَإِنَّ الذِّكْرى تَنْفَعُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (And admonish, for admonition indeed benefits the faithful) was revealed, our souls were comforted." Its meaning is: Exhort with the Quran those of your people who have believed—for the reminder benefits them. Narrated from al-Kalbi.

I say: It was narrated in al-Durr al-Manthur, and its meaning was also narrated from Ibn Rahwayh and Ibn Mardawayh from him (peace be upon him).

In al-Tawhid, with its chain of transmission from Ibn Abi Umayr who said: I said to Abu al-Hasan Musa ibn Ja'far (peace be upon him): "What is the meaning of the saying of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): 'Act, for everyone is facilitated for that for which he was created'?" He replied: "إن الله عز وجل خلق الجن والإنس ليعبدوه ـ ولم يخلقهم ليعصوه وذلك قوله عز وجل : «وَما خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ» فيسر كلا لما خلق له ـ فويل لمن استحب العمى على الهدى" — "Indeed Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created the jinn and the humans to worship Him—and He did not create them to disobey Him. That is His saying, Mighty and Majestic: 'I did not create the jinn and the humans except that they may worship Me.' So He facilitated each for that for which He created him—so woe to him who prefers blindness over guidance."

In al-Ilal, with its chain of transmission to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: "Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon him) came out to his companions and said: 'إن الله عز وجل ما خلق العباد إلا ليعرفوه ، فإذا عرفوه عبدوه ، فإذا عبدوه استغنوا بعبادته عن عبادة من سواه' — 'Indeed Allah, Mighty and Majestic, did not create the servants except that they may know Him. For when they know Him, they worship Him; and when they worship Him, they become independent through His worship from the worship of any besides Him.'"

In the same source, with its chain of transmission to Abu Basir who said: I asked Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) about the saying of Allah, Mighty and Majestic: وَما خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ. He said: "خلقهم ليأمرهم بالعبادة" — "He created them to command them with worship."

I say: Al-Qummi narrated the like of it in his Tafsir as a disconnected (مرسلا) and unnamed (مضمرا) report. What clarifies the meaning of these narrations has already passed in the commentary on the verse: that there are sequential purposes: religious obligation (التكليف), worship (العبادة), and gnosis (المعرفة).

In Tafsir al-Ayyashi, from Ya'qub ibn Sa'id from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: I asked him about the saying of Allah: وَما خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ. He said: "He created them for worship." I said: "[What about] His saying: وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ ـ إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ وَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ (...they will not cease to differ, except those on whom your Lord has mercy—and for that did He create them)?" He replied: "This was revealed after that."

I say: That is, "...they will not cease..." etc., was revealed after "I did not create..." etc. He intends abrogation (النسخ). In Tafsir al-Qummi: "In another hadith: it is abrogated by His saying: '...they will not cease to differ'." What is meant by abrogation (النسخ) here is clarification (البيان) and the removal of ambiguity, not abrogation in the technical sense. This meaning has frequently occurred in their speech (peace be upon them), as we pointed out in the commentary on His saying, the Exalted: ما نَنْسَخْ مِنْ آيَةٍ أَوْ نُنْسِها "Any verse that We abrogate or cause to be forgotten..." (2:106).

The meaning is that the highest purpose is the specific mercy (الرحمة الخاصة) resulting from worship, which is the specific felicity (السعادة) associated with gnosis.

In al-Tahdhib, with its chain of transmission to Sudayr who said: I said to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): "What is upon a man in seeking provision?" He replied: "إذا فتحت بابك وبسطت بساطك ـ فقد قضيت ما عليك" — "When you have opened your door and spread your mat—then you have fulfilled what is upon you."

— Completed, and praise be to Allah —
